---
title: 'Rural employment trends by Census Region: West leads recovery'
description: Relative change in rural employment since 2007, broken out by the four
  Census regions.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-3-2-rural-region-emp-change-since-gr-lc/
categories: []
tags: []
image: 3.2_rural_region_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: 'Rural employment trends by Census Region: West leads recovery'
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Facets the rural employment index by Census region (Northeast, Midwest, South, West), revealing that recovery from the Great Recession varies substantially by geography.](./3.2_rural_region_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

Facets the rural employment index by Census region (Northeast, Midwest, South, West), revealing that recovery from the Great Recession varies substantially by geography.

## Key Findings

- The West leads rural employment recovery, crossing back above 2007 levels earlier than other regions.
- The Midwest and South show more moderate recoveries with persistent gaps below baseline.
- The Northeast has the weakest rural employment trajectory of the four regions.
- Regional divergence accelerated after COVID-19 as migration shifted labor supply across geographies.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/emp_change_lc.R` in the producing project.
